mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates demand outpacing supply capacity, triggering inflationary pressures and monetary tightening expectations that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power (interaction term p-value > 0.10) or forward returns are positive in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of stable industrial output or when forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 200 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that compress real corporate margins and trigger defensive portfolio rebalancing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) or positive returns occur in < 45% of growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand that translates directly into higher quarterly earnings estimates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or the joint signal fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% across multiple recessionary periods.
